sections.paragraph:
pl. בְּקָרִים.-

(1) morning, daybreak, dawn [“and even before light, Rth 3:14 ”], so called from the breaking forth of light; see the root No. 2. (Arab. بَكَرُ, بُكْرَةُ id.; compare the root בָּכַר No. 1 ). אֹור בֹּקֶר morning light, 2Sa 23:4. In acc. adv. in the morning (like the Arab. بَكَرًا), Psa 5:4 more often בַּבֹּקֶר in the morning, Gen 19:27 and poetically לַבֹּקֶר Psa 30:6 59:17 , which is elsewhere עַד בֹּקֶר Deu 16:4 Psa 130:6. Distributively בַּבֹּקֶר בַּבֹּקֶר Exo 16:21 30:7 36:3 Lev 6:5 לַבֹּקֶר לַבֹּקֶר 1Ch 9:27 לַבְּקָרִים Psa 73:14 101:8 Isa 33:2 Lam 3:23 לִבְקָרִים Job 7:18, every morning; metaph. in the morning, i.e. dawn of prosperity, Job 11:17.

(2) Specially the next morning, Exo 29:34 Lev 19:13 22:30 Num 9:12 Jud. 6:31 , אֲשֶׁר יָרִיב לֹו יוּמַת עַד־הַבֹּקֶר “whoever will plead for him, let him be put to death before to-morrow morning” (Vulg. “antequam lux crastinaveniat;” LXX. ἕως πρωΐ). Hence to-morrow, i.q. מָחָר (compare the word אֶמֶשׁ); and adv. to-morrow, Exo 16:7 Num 16:5 (comp. verse 16 Num 16:16), i.q. בַּבֹּקֶר 1Sa 19:2 used for presently, Psa 5:4 (in the former hemistich); 90:14 143:8 לַבֹּקֶר id., Psa 49:15.
